title: Goldfish Size Records
description: What are the maximum recorded lengths for the different goldfish varieties and which individual fish hold those records?
narrative: The user wants factual information on the largest sizes ever documented for each major goldfish type (e.g., common/comet, fancy varieties such as Oranda, Ryukin, Ranchu, etc.). Relevant results should list breed‑specific size ranges, name the record‑holding fish, give their measured lengths, include lifespan or age if noted, and cite credible sources or reports supporting the records.

title: WiFi vs Bluetooth
description: How do Wi‑Fi dongles compare to Bluetooth dongles in terms of speed, range, cost, and suitable use cases for file transfer?
narrative: The user wants to decide which wireless technology to use for connecting two computers. Relevant documents should discuss the maximum transfer speeds (e.g., Wi‑Fi Direct up to 250 Mbps vs Bluetooth 4.0 up to 25 Mbps), the typical operating range (≈100 m for Wi‑Fi, ≈10 m for Bluetooth), price ranges of the dongles ($3‑$30), and common applications such as networking, printing, or file sharing. Documents that only mention one technology without comparative details are not sufficient.

title: Telegram and US Entry
description: What role did the Zimmermann Telegram play in the United States' decision to enter World War I compared to other factors?
narrative: The user seeks sources that explain the content, interception, and diplomatic fallout of the Zimmermann Telegram and assess its significance relative to other influences such as unrestricted submarine warfare, the sinking of the Lusitania, and President Wilson’s ideological goals. Relevant documents must discuss the telegram’s provenance, the British decision to release it to the press, Mexican reactions, and how historians evaluate its impact on the U.S. declaration of war.

title: Declaratory Judgment Requirements
description: What are the legal requirements and limitations for obtaining a declaratory judgment to quiet title in U.S. courts?
narrative: The user wants a clear explanation of when a declaratory judgment can be used to remove cloud on title, focusing on its statutory (non‑equitable) nature, the necessity of an actual, justiciable controversy, the case‑or‑controversy and jurisdictional prerequisites, and how these elements compare to a quiet‑title action. Relevant documents must discuss declaratory judgments as statutory remedies, the requirement for a concrete adverse‑interest dispute, and the procedural similarity to quiet‑title proceedings.

title: RLQ Pain Causes
description: What conditions can cause lower right abdominal (right lower quadrant) pain and how can they be differentiated?
narrative: The user seeks a comprehensive overview of medical disorders that present with right lower quadrant pain, including kidney infection, kidney stones, appendicitis, pelvic pathology (e.g., ovarian cyst, endometriosis, pelvic abscess), gastrointestinal issues (e.g., IBD, IBS), and musculoskeletal problems. Relevant documents should list each condition, describe characteristic accompanying symptoms (such as groin or back pain, painful urination, pain migration from the navel, abdominal mass, or anorectal pain), and note distinguishing clinical features that help differentiate among these causes.

title: Social Determinants Overview
description: What are the primary social determinants of health and how do they influence individual and population health outcomes?
narrative: The user wants a concise yet comprehensive explanation of the widely accepted social determinants of health, including categories such as biology/genetics, individual behavior, social environment, and physical environment, along with examples (e.g., income, education, housing, discrimination). Relevant material should define these determinants, illustrate how they affect health status, and discuss the interplay between socioeconomic factors and health access.

title: Legionella Species
description: Which Legionella species are known to cause Legionnaires' disease and how do they differ in prevalence and clinical presentation?
narrative: The user is seeking a comprehensive overview of the bacterial agents responsible for Legionnaires' disease, including Legionella pneumophila and less common species such as L. longbeachae, L. feeleii, L. micdadei, and L. anisa. Relevant documents should identify each species, note that over 90% of cases are due to L. pneumophila, and describe any distinct clinical features (e.g., severity, association with Pontiac fever) or epidemiological patterns. Documents that only mention generic pneumonia causes without specifying Legionella species are not relevant.

title: Montego Bay Travel Timing
description: When is the optimal time of year to travel to Montego Bay, Jamaica, considering typical weather conditions, rainfall, and hurricane risk?
narrative: The user wants guidance on the best months to visit Montego Bay for a pleasant vacation, focusing on dry-season weather, comfortable temperatures, minimal rainfall, and low likelihood of hurricanes or tropical storms. Relevant documents are those that provide monthly climate averages, live weather updates, and historical storm information, especially the recommendation that December through April offers the most favorable conditions.

title: Differentiating Speech Disorders
description: What clinical signs and characteristics distinguish dysarthria from childhood apraxia of speech (developmental verbal dyspraxia) and cluttering in children?
narrative: The user is seeking guidance on how to correctly identify and separate dysarthria from other pediatric speech and fluency disorders such as developmental verbal dyspraxia and cluttering. Relevant documents must describe the definition, causes, and observable speech features of dysarthria, as well as comparable information on cluttering and childhood apraxia of speech, allowing the user to compare symptom profiles and diagnostic criteria.

title: Columbia River Claim
description: When did Captain Robert Gray claim the Columbia River for the United States and what events led to that claim?
narrative: The user seeks information about the 1792 expedition in which Captain Robert Gray entered the mouth of the Columbia River, named it after his ship, and formally claimed the region for the United States. Relevant material should describe the date (May 11, 1792), the extent of his exploration (up to 20 miles), the naming of Grays Bay, the context of American fur‑trading interests, and any subsequent related explorations such as Lewis and Clark (1805) and the establishment of Astoria (1811). Documents that only mention the river without reference to Gray’s claim, or that discuss later history without the 1792 event, are not relevant.

title: Sous Vide Short Ribs
description: What cooking time and finishing method should I use for sous vide short ribs to maximize tenderness while still achieving a crispy, caramelized exterior?
narrative: The user is looking for practical guidance on preparing short ribs using the sous vide technique. Relevant information must address the recommended minimum and optional extended cooking durations (e.g., 48 hours for tenderness, up to 72 hours for ultra‑tender results) and describe high‑heat finishing options such as searing in a skillet, grilling with wood chips, or applying a BBQ glaze. Documents that discuss flavor variations, the need for a final high‑heat step, and the trade‑off between tenderness and crispness are considered relevant.

title: Flea Survival Timeline
description: How long can adult fleas survive without a blood meal and what implications does this have for post‑vacation pest control?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive information on the maximum duration adult fleas can live without feeding, including their ability to hibernate, as well as related aspects of the flea life cycle such as egg‑laying frequency and environmental persistence. Relevant content must describe flea longevity without a host, the conditions under which they can survive for months, and how these factors influence effective flea control measures after a house has been vacant for an extended period.

title: Contour Plowing Erosion
description: How does contour plowing reduce soil erosion and what evidence supports its effectiveness compared to other tillage methods?
narrative: The user seeks information that explains the principle of contour plowing, describes how it slows water runoff and preserves topsoil, and provides evidence or case studies demonstrating its erosion‑control benefits relative to alternative practices such as straight‑line plowing, irrigation changes, or grazing. Relevant documents should define contour plowing, detail its mechanism (e.g., furrows following elevation contours), and cite examples or research showing reduced soil loss.

title: Salvation Army Origins
description: When and how did William Booth establish the Salvation Army, and what role did his wife Catherine play in its early development?
narrative: The user wants a concise historical account of the founding of the Salvation Army, including the year it began, the key events that led to its creation (such as Booth’s 1865 evangelistic meetings and his shift from pulpit to street ministry), and the contributions of Catherine Booth as co‑founder. Relevant material must contain specific dates, descriptions of early activities, and references to both William and Catherine Booth’s involvement.

title: Active Continental Margin
description: What geological features and processes define an active continental margin where oceanic crust subducts beneath continental crust?
narrative: The user wants a comprehensive explanation of the characteristic phenomena of active continental margins, including subduction, trench formation, volcanic mountain chains, deep earthquakes, magma generation, and uplift of mountains. Relevant documents should describe convergent margins, subduction mechanics, the creation of oceanic trenches and inland volcanic arcs, and provide examples such as the western coast of Canada where the North American plate overrides the Pacific plate.

title: RN to BSN Benefits
description: What are the career and salary advantages of completing an RN-to-BSN program compared to remaining with an ADN?
narrative: The user is looking for a comparative overview of RN-to-BSN completion programs versus ADN qualifications, focusing on salary differences, typical job levels, required credits and program length, and additional career growth opportunities. Relevant documents should discuss how RN salaries compare across degrees, hiring practices that favor BSN graduates, and the professional benefits of earning a BSN.

title: Home Ventilation Types
description: What are the distinctions, benefits, and drawbacks of invasive versus non‑invasive mechanical ventilation for chronic respiratory failure in the home setting?
narrative: The user wants a detailed comparison of long‑term home ventilation methods, covering invasive (tracheostomy tube) and non‑invasive (mask‑based CPAP/BiPAP) systems. Relevant material should explain how each delivers air and oxygen, the role of pressure support (e.g., PEEP), the equipment components (ventilator, mask, cannula, etc.), patient comfort and usability, and typical clinical indications for choosing one approach over the other.

title: Trapezoid Midsegment
description: How do you calculate the midsegment length of a trapezoid and use it to solve related area and perimeter problems?
narrative: The user seeks a clear explanation of the trapezoid midsegment (midline), including its definition, the formula that it equals half the sum of the bases, and how it is parallel to the bases. Relevant information should cover example calculations of midsegment length, connections to finding areas and perimeters of trapezoids, and related concepts such as isosceles trapezoid angle properties and classification of quadrilaterals. Documents that provide step‑by‑step problem solutions, definitions, and formulas for midsegments, as well as related geometry problems (e.g., kite side lengths, quadrilateral types), are considered relevant.

title: AML Analyst Role
description: What are the core duties, reporting structure, and required experience for a senior anti‑money‑laundering analyst?
narrative: The user wants a comprehensive overview of a senior AML analyst’s daily responsibilities, including guidance to staff, transaction‑monitoring analysis, reporting to a Scenario Review Manager, and the broader legal context of AML work. Relevant content must describe specific job tasks, required experience, and how the role fits within AML compliance programs.

title: Thai Flag Meaning
description: What do the colors and stripe pattern of Thailand’s national flag symbolize, and how are they connected to the country's history, monarchy, and religion?
narrative: The user wants a comprehensive explanation of each color (red, white, blue) and the arrangement of the five stripes on the Thai flag, including references to national identity, Buddhism, the royal family, and historical ties such as World War I allies. Relevant documents should explicitly describe the flag’s design, color symbolism, and any official naming (e.g., Thong Trairong).

title: Helicopter Weather Data
description: Which aviation weather products are provided to helicopter pilots by cockpit systems such as GDL‑69 and WSI, and how do they compare?
narrative: The user wants a detailed inventory of the weather information delivered to a helicopter cockpit by systems like GDL‑69 and WSI, covering graphical and textual products such as NEXRAD, METAR, TAF, SIGMET, AIRMET, TFR, PIREP, icing levels, winds and temperatures aloft, precipitation type, lightning strikes, storm‑cell data, and city forecasts. Relevant documents must list these items for each system and highlight any differences in content or format.

title: Concrete Flooring Cost
description: What are the typical material and installation price ranges for concrete tile flooring and polished concrete floors for a residential project?
narrative: The user wants a budgeting overview for concrete flooring options. Relevant passages must list cost estimates for concrete tile (including discount, premium, and luxury grades) expressed per 500‑square‑foot area, as well as cost ranges for polished concrete expressed per square foot (including basic 800‑grit polish and options with staining or decorative scoring). Information about how the finish level (e.g., 800‑grit, glossy) affects price should also be included.

title: Swiss Culinary Staples
description: What traditional foods, regional dishes, and iconic food products (e.g., chocolate, cheese, breakfast items) are associated with Switzerland?
narrative: The user seeks a comprehensive overview of Swiss cuisine, including typical breakfast components, regional specialties such as Pastetli and Zurcher Geschnetzeltes, and famous Swiss food products like chocolate and various cheeses. Relevant documents should mention these foods, describe their characteristics, and situate them within Swiss culinary traditions.

title: Warm-Blooded Fish
description: Which fish species are warm‑blooded and what are the mechanisms and temperature ranges of their thermoregulation?
narrative: The user is seeking information on fish that can elevate their body temperature above that of the surrounding water, unlike typical cold‑blooded fish. Relevant documents should identify species such as tuna, the Pacific salmon shark, shortfin mako, and porbeagle, describe how they generate and retain heat, and provide quantitative details (e.g., temperature differentials of 7–10 °C). Documents that only discuss general fish anatomy, gill respiration, or cold‑blooded characteristics are not relevant.

title: DME Provider vs Supplier
description: What are the distinctions between a Durable Medical Equipment (DME) provider and a DME supplier, including their roles, coverage definitions, and special‑exception items?
narrative: The user wants clear, authoritative information that explains how a DME provider differs from a DME supplier in terms of responsibilities, ordering processes, and coverage under health plans. Relevant content should define DME, prosthetics, orthotics, and medical supplies, outline the provider versus supplier services, and note any special‑exception coverage rules for items that do not strictly meet the DME definition.

title: Alternative Splicing Overview
description: Explain how exons and introns participate in alternative splicing and how this process contributes to protein diversity in humans.
narrative: The user seeks a comprehensive description of alternative splicing, including the definitions of exons and introns, the mechanistic steps (introns removal, exon joining, inclusion/exclusion of specific exons), quantitative context (average number of exons and introns per human gene), the prevalence of alternative splicing across the human genome, and the role of regulatory proteins such as YB‑1. Relevant documents must cover at least one of these aspects and provide clear, factual information that connects exon/intron structure to the generation of multiple protein isoforms.

title: Visceral Definition
description: What does the term 'visceral' mean, and how is it used to describe gut-level emotional reactions, especially in language and bilingual contexts?
narrative: The user wants a clear explanation of the word 'visceral', including its literal (internal organ) origin and its figurative sense of deep, gut‑felt emotions. Relevant material should distinguish visceral reactions from purely emotional or rational responses, provide examples such as the visceral impact of swearing in one's native language, and relate the concept to similar ideas like instinct.

title: Xylem Cell Types
description: What are the different cell types that make up primary and secondary xylem in plants, and what are their specific roles in water and mineral transport?
narrative: The user seeks a detailed overview of the cellular composition of xylem, distinguishing primary from secondary xylem and describing the function of each cell type (e.g., tracheids, vessel elements, parenchyma, fibers). Relevant information must mention these cell types, explain their roles in conduction and structural support, and note the context of primary versus secondary growth.

title: RSA Authentication
description: How does RSA provide authentication for Diffie‑Hellman exchanges in IPsec/IKE VPNs?
narrative: The user seeks detailed information on the use of RSA signatures to authenticate Diffie‑Hellman key exchanges in IKE/IPsec, including protocol mechanisms, security benefits, and real‑world implementations (e.g., Cisco routers or firewalls).

title: Post‑Soviet Alliances
description: What international organizations were created after the Soviet Union’s dissolution, and how do their origins, purposes, and memberships compare to earlier bodies such as the League of Nations?
narrative: The user seeks a concise overview of post‑1991 intergovernmental entities—including the Commonwealth of Independent States (CIS) and the United Nations' role after 1991—as well as a comparison with pre‑World‑War‑II organizations like the League of Nations. Relevant passages should describe each organization’s founding date, member composition, primary objectives (e.g., collective security, cooperation, peace), and any notable historical ties or contrasts with earlier institutions.

title: Losartan LVH
description: What evidence exists that losartan reduces left ventricular hypertrophy and improves diastolic function in patients with chronic hypertension?
narrative: The user seeks information on the therapeutic impact of losartan on hypertension‑induced left ventricular hypertrophy (LVH) and associated diastolic dysfunction. Relevant documents should discuss losartan’s use for high blood pressure, its role in decreasing stroke risk in patients with LVH, and any data showing reversal or mitigation of LV hypertrophy or improvement in cardiac compliance. Sources that differentiate LVH due to hypertension from other causes (e.g., HCM, amyloidosis) and that describe clinical outcomes or mechanisms are considered relevant.

title: Baker Act Release
description: What are the procedural steps, time limits, and requirements for releasing someone placed under the Florida Baker Act, including the 72‑hour examination period and special provisions for minors?
narrative: The user seeks a comprehensive explanation of the Baker Act process, specifically how the 72‑hour involuntary examination period is calculated, what actions trigger a new act, and any mandatory conditions (such as family therapy for minors) that must be satisfied before release. Relevant documents must describe the Act’s definition, the role of judges, law enforcement, physicians, and mental‑health professionals, the 72‑hour rule, the 2005 outpatient placement reform, and any special requirements for releasing a minor.

title: Three Percenter Identification
description: What indicators or criteria can be used to determine whether a person is truly a Three Percenter?
narrative: The user seeks reliable information that outlines the defining traits, behaviors, symbols, and self‑identification methods associated with the Three Percenters movement, including any commonly cited checklists or “ways to tell” that distinguish genuine members from others. Relevant documents should discuss the movement’s origins, core beliefs, and practical markers (e.g., language, attire, affiliations, public statements) that signal authentic affiliation.

title: Military Suicide Factors
description: What mental health conditions and service‑related factors are driving the rise in suicide rates among U.S. active‑duty personnel and veterans, and what prevention resources are available?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive information on the causes behind the increasing suicide rates in the U.S. military and veteran populations, including statistics, the role of PTSD, anxiety, depression, trauma, homelessness, and other risk factors, as well as descriptions of existing prevention programs, support organizations, and government reports. Relevant documents must mention suicide statistics, comparisons to combat deaths, prevalence of PTSD among Iraq/Afghanistan veterans, links between anxiety/mood disorders and suicide, and list or describe resources such as SAMHSA initiatives, VA reports, and nonprofit support networks.

title: Theraderm Effectiveness
description: What evidence supports the anti‑aging claims of Theraderm's Skin Renewal System and how do consumer reviews reflect its actual performance?
narrative: The user wants to assess whether Theraderm's clinically‑promoted Skin Renewal System lives up to its marketed benefits (reducing fine lines, improving rough skin, and addressing sun damage). Relevant information includes the company's background, the product's claimed ingredients and benefits, and independent consumer‑driven ratings that may contradict or confirm those claims. Documents that discuss Theraderm's founding, its dermatologist endorsement strategy, the specific features of the Skin Renewal System, and any reported consumer satisfaction scores are considered relevant.

title: Famciclovir Dosing
description: What are the recommended dosing regimens of famciclovir for shingles, genital herpes, and cold sores, and how do they compare to other antiviral treatments?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive information on famciclovir dosing for each of its approved indications (shingles, genital herpes, and cold sores), including dosage amounts, frequency, treatment duration, and timing of initiation (e.g., within 72 hours of rash onset). Relevant documents should also address how famciclovir’s dosing and efficacy compare to the other commonly used antivirals acyclovir and valacyclovir, noting prescription status and any differences in severity or duration of symptoms.

title: Ecological Anthropology Definition
description: What is the definition and scope of ecological anthropology as a subfield of anthropology?
narrative: The user wants a thorough explanation of ecological anthropology, covering its historical emergence in the 1960s as a response to cultural ecology, its focus on cultural adaptations to environments, and how it studies the relationships between human populations and their biophysical surroundings. Relevant material should include definitions of environment, the role of culture in human‑environment interaction, and the broader anthropological context.

title: Synaptic Transmission
description: What are the step‑by‑step cellular events that occur from the arrival of an action potential at the axon terminal to neurotransmitter release across the synaptic cleft?
narrative: The user wants a comprehensive description of the physiological sequence at a chemical synapse. Relevant information must cover the depolarization of the presynaptic axon terminal, opening of voltage‑gated calcium channels, calcium influx, vesicle fusion and neurotransmitter release, as well as the structure and role of the synaptic cleft and the postsynaptic membrane (including dendritic reception). Documents that detail each of these stages and the associated neuronal components are considered relevant.

title: Paris Airport Comparison
description: What are the key differences between Paris‑Charles de Gaulle (CDG) and Paris‑Orly (ORY) regarding location, traffic volume, and airline service?
narrative: The user seeks a concise side‑by‑side overview of the two primary Paris airports. Relevant content must provide details on each airport’s distance from central Paris, passenger or flight statistics, the main airlines operating there, and any notable rankings (e.g., busiest airport in Europe). Only information that directly contrasts CDG and Orly on these criteria is considered relevant.

title: Monotonic Function Definition
description: What is the precise definition of a monotonic (monotone) function in calculus, and how does it relate to increasing and decreasing behavior on intervals?
narrative: The user seeks authoritative explanations of monotonic functions, including the formal definition (increments never change sign), the distinction between monotonic increasing and decreasing, and illustrative examples such as y = x^2 on different intervals. Relevant documents must contain clear definitions, properties of monotonicity, and descriptions of how the graph behaves (rising or falling) across intervals.

title: Spruce vs Fir
description: What characteristics can be used to reliably tell a spruce tree apart from a true fir?
narrative: The user is seeking practical, observable traits for distinguishing spruce trees from fir trees in the field. Relevant information must describe diagnostic features such as needle shape (four‑sided vs flat), needle texture (stiff/prickly vs soft/flexible), the presence of a woody peg at the needle base, cone orientation (spruce cones hanging downward, fir cones standing upright), cone texture (hard vs soft), and any distinctive scent. Documents that provide any of these identification cues are considered relevant.

title: Hydrogen Liquefaction Temperature
description: What temperature does hydrogen liquefy (turn from gas to liquid) under standard conditions?
narrative: The user seeks the exact temperature at which hydrogen transitions from a gaseous to a liquid state, ideally with values in both Fahrenheit and Celsius and an indication that the condition is standard temperature and pressure; any source that states hydrogen's liquefaction (boiling) point, phase‑change temperature, or related thermodynamic data for hydrogen will satisfy the information need.

title: McDouble vs Double Cheeseburger
description: What are the differences in ingredients, price, and calories between McDonald's McDouble and Double Cheeseburger?
narrative: The user wants a clear comparison of the McDouble and the Double Cheeseburger, focusing on the number of cheese slices, any other topping differences, typical price points (especially on the Dollar Menu) and the calorie content of each. Relevant documents must mention cheese quantity, price variations (including the $1‑plus reality of the Dollar Menu), and calorie information for the McDouble. Sources that only discuss one item without these comparative details are not relevant.
